test_cases.title: Schema injection via --append-system-prompt temp file
test_cases.description: Verify that when agent() is called with schema, a temp file is created and --append-system-prompt is passed to the subprocess
steps: Create workflow script with agent('test', {schema: {type:'object', properties:{result:{type:'string'}}, required:['result']}})
Verify temp file content includes structured-output call instruction

test_cases.title: Schema with special characters preserved
test_cases.description: Schema containing quotes, newlines, backslashes is correctly written to temp file without corruption
steps: Create schema with special chars: {description: 'test "quoted" value\nwith newline'}
Read temp file and verify JSON matches original schema

test_cases.title: Structured output retry on first failure
test_cases.description: When subprocess returns no SO and no tool call, system retries once automatically
steps: Mock runPiProcess to return no SO + no tool call on first call, valid SO on second call
Verify final result is success with correct parsedOutput
Verify runPiProcess was called exactly twice

test_cases.title: Blind-spot: tool calls but no structured-output on exit
test_cases.description: When subprocess calls other tools but not structured-output and exits with code 0, return failure
steps: Mock runPiProcess to return hasToolCall=true, no SO, exitCode=0

test_cases.title: CC-style phases with title/detail objects
test_cases.description: config-loader correctly parses phases as (string | {title, detail?})[]
steps: Create script with meta = { phases: [{title:'Review'}, {title:'Fix', detail:'fix issues'}] }
Verify phases array contains two objects with correct title and detail fields

test_cases.title: args alias in worker-script
test_cases.description: Worker script exposes args as alias for $ARGS
steps: Verify args.maxIterations is accessible in script context

test_cases.title: Implicit phase propagation to trace node
test_cases.description: phase('Review') followed by agent() sets trace node phase to 'Review'

test_cases.title: Explicit phase override in agent() call
steps: Verify opts.phase sent to main thread is 'Fix'

test_cases.title: parallel() with thunk array
test_cases.description: parallel([() => agent('t1'), () => agent('t2')]) executes both thunks concurrently
steps: Build worker script with parallel([() => agent('task1'), () => agent('task2')])
Execute and verify two agent-call messages are sent
Verify both calls run concurrently (not sequentially)

test_cases.title: pipeline() cartesian product happy path
test_cases.description: pipeline([1,2,3], stage1, stage2) runs 3 items each through 2 stages
steps: Call pipeline([1,2,3], x => agent('process '+x), r => agent('verify '+r))
Verify 6 agent calls total (3 items × 2 stages)

test_cases.title: pipeline() error isolation
test_cases.description: Single item failure in pipeline produces null, other items unaffected

test_cases.title: budget.spent() returns current token consumption
test_cases.description: After agent completes, budget.spent() reflects accumulated usage
steps: After first agent completes with usage.input = 500
After second agent completes with usage.input = 300

test_cases.title: Backward compatibility with Pi-format scripts
test_cases.description: Existing Pi scripts with string[] phases and $ARGS still work
steps: Create script: const meta = {name:'test', phases:['Phase1']}; module.exports = {meta, execute: async ({agent}) => { await agent('hello'); }}
